reference: ref_f32_bits[i] == (bf16_u16_hex[i] << 16); bf16 = high 16 bits of the IEEE-754 f32 encoding

provenance.method: bf16 -> f32 is the exact bit-widening f32::from_bits(u32::from(u16) << 16): bf16 stores the high 16 bits of the IEEE-754 binary32 encoding, so widening appends 16 zero low bits. Lossless, no rounding, bit-deterministic for every input including +/-0, subnormals, +/-Inf and NaN payloads.
provenance.pin_equivalence: Identical to the pinned llama.cpp (acd79d603) ggml_bf16_to_fp32 by definition. The mapping is definitional, so the reference bits are enumerated from the definition rather than captured from a binary run -- running the pin for a lossless widening would be theater. The in-test golden self-check asserts ref_f32_bits[i] == (bf16_u16 << 16) independently.
provenance.campaign: BASALT D-B6 (2026-07-17): BF16 admitted to the runnable-lane covered quant set as an exact-decode type; M-B5 exit condition (a) -- a committed BF16 dequant-parity fixture.
